description: A vertically-stacked three-panel slogan graphic produced by the U.S. Department of Labor. Each of the three horizontal panels shows a desaturated, grainy, washed-out black-and-white photograph of derelict American industrial landscapes evoking the post-industrial 'Rust Belt': the top panel shows an abandoned factory complex with two tall brick smokestacks across a barren field; the middle panel shows a rusting elevated water tower beside a large derelict multi-story brick mill or warehouse; the bottom panel shows a low industrial building beside disused railroad tracks and utility poles. The images are deliberately treated with film-grain, dust specks, scratches and a dark, gloomy, decayed aesthetic. Bold white sans-serif capital text is overlaid centered across the three panels in sequence, reading 'GLOBALISM' / 'HAS' / 'FAILED.' so the message is read top-to-bottom. The propaganda technique pairs imagery of industrial ruin and economic abandonment with a one-word indictment of 'globalism' to argue that free-trade/offshoring policies hollowed out American manufacturing. The accompanying tweet frames this as a call to put 'AMERICA FIRST,' end offshoring and the prioritization of foreign labor. Target audience: working-class Americans, manufacturing/blue-collar workers and economic-nationalist supporters of the administration's protectionist agenda.
